title: Cottage Cheese Ice Cream
metaDescription: No-churn cottage cheese ice cream with 18g of protein per serving — just 4 ingredients blended smooth and frozen. No ice cream maker needed.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: cottage cheese ice cream, protein ice cream, no-churn ice cream
ingredients: Optional mix-ins: chocolate chips, fresh berries, crushed graham crackers

steps.name: Blend until completely smooth
steps.text: Add cottage cheese, honey, vanilla, and salt to a blender or food processor. Blend on high for 1-2 minutes, scraping down the sides, until totally smooth with zero curd texture remaining.

steps.name: Fold in mix-ins
steps.text: If using, fold in chocolate chips or crushed graham crackers by hand — don't blend them in.

steps.name: Freeze
steps.text: Pour into a loaf pan or airtight container. Freeze for at least 4 hours, or until firm.

steps.name: Scoop and serve
steps.text: Let sit at room temperature for 5-10 minutes before scooping for the best soft-serve texture.

tips.question: Why is my cottage cheese ice cream icy instead of creamy?
tips.answer: Under-blending is the most common cause — the mixture needs to be 100% smooth with no visible curds before freezing, or you'll get an icy texture instead of creamy. Blend longer than you think you need to.

tips.question: Do I need an ice cream maker?
tips.answer: No — this is a no-churn recipe. The honey and full-fat cottage cheese keep it soft enough to scoop straight from the freezer without an ice cream maker.

tips.question: Can I use low-fat cottage cheese?
tips.answer: You can, but full-fat gives a noticeably creamier result. Low-fat versions freeze slightly harder and icier.

tips.question: How long does it keep in the freezer?
tips.answer: Up to 2 weeks in an airtight container. Texture is best in the first week.
